page: Microsoft Azure FX24mds_v2 is a memory-optimized virtual machine powered by the Microsoft Hyper-V hypervisor. It features 24 dedicated vCPUs mapped from 12 physical cores of an Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at a clock speed of 4.2 GHz. The x86_64 architecture includes a 260 MB L3 cache and supports advanced instruction sets such as AVX-512 and Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX) for accelerated matrix operations.

This server type is provisioned with 504.0 GB of system memory, yielding a high resource density of 21.0 GB of RAM per CPU core. For local storage requirements, it includes a bundled 1417 GB local disk and supports Premium Storage configurations. No graphics processing units are included in this instance configuration.

In benchmark evaluations, the FX24mds_v2 demonstrates top-tier performance in single-core operations, with Geekbench single-core and single-threaded gzip compression tests scoring in the top 10% of tested instances. Image and text processing also rank in the top tier. Multi-core performance, such as Geekbench multi-core and bzip3 decompression, falls into the strong top 25% tier. Memory bandwidth reaches up to 2070.94 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 166.75 GB/sec for uncached large blocks.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the high memory-to-vCPU ratio provides a dense resource profile suitable for memory-intensive workloads without requiring the purchase of excess compute cores. The combination of high-frequency Intel Xeon processors, massive RAM capacity, and local storage makes this instance well-suited for large relational databases, in-memory caching layers, data analytics, and memory-bound enterprise applications.
description: Microsoft Azure FX24mds_v2 is a memory-optimized virtual machine configured with 24 dedicated vCPUs and 504.0 GB of RAM, running on an Intel Xeon 8573C processor at 4.2 GHz. Built on the x86_64 architecture under Microsoft Hyper-V, it features 12 physical cores, a 260 MB L3 cache, and supports advanced instruction sets like AVX-512 and Intel AMX. The instance includes 1417 GB of local storage but lacks dedicated GPUs. Benchmark data highlights top-tier single-core performance, with Geekbench single-core and single-threaded gzip compression scoring in the top 10%. Multi-core capabilities and static web serving throughput rank in the top 25%. This high memory-to-vCPU ratio makes the FX24mds_v2 highly cost-efficient for memory-intensive workloads, such as large-scale databases, in-memory data structures, and enterprise applications requiring substantial memory capacity per core.
og_description: Microsoft Azure FX24mds_v2 is a memory-optimized VM featuring 24 vCPUs, 504 GB RAM, and a 1417 GB local disk, delivering top-tier single-core performance for memory-intensive enterprise workloads.
meta_description: Microsoft Azure FX24mds_v2 is a memory-optimized VM with 24 vCPUs, 504 GB RAM, and 1417 GB local storage, ideal for database workloads.
tagline: A high-frequency, memory-intensive virtual machine featuring a 1:21 vCPU-to-RAM ratio and integrated local storage for demanding database workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 24-vCPU Intel Xeon 8573C processor running at 4.2 GHz
Equipped with 504.0 GB of RAM, providing 21.0 GB of memory per core
Includes 1417 GB of local storage with Premium Storage capabilities
Top-tier single-core performance in Geekbench and gzip compression benchmarks
Optimized for memory-intensive databases, caching layers, and enterprise applications
